text: You read a poem about fire that doesn't need permission. About someone who built a cathedral out of something no one else thought was real, and filled it with everyone they could find, and called it a literature club.

speaker: Grubwin
text: You can't respond until everyone finishes, Vermia.

speaker: Vermia
text: *very quietly* I'm not responding I'm just — leaking a little —
After the exchange, during tea, Vermia finds you by the bookshelf.

speaker: Vermia
text: I want you to know that poem meant more to me than you probably intended it to. The bit about the cathedral. I've never — no one's ever put it that way. What I was doing. What this is.

speaker: Vermia
text: Scatha definitely sent you. I just want that on record.
